codeReview.summary: The patch does add the missing retry primitive for the narrow case where a skipped refresh is followed by the same agent eventually exposing `agentDid`, and the new unit suite exercises that poller directly. But it still does not close the original blocker for restore-based initialize flows: `restoreFromMnemonic()` still calls `agent.initialize(...)` and then `refreshIdentities()` without ever calling `agent.start()`, while upstream only assigns `agentDid` inside `start()`. In that real path the new poller has nothing to observe and just times out after 2s, so restored wallets can still reach Main with stale or empty identities until a later manual refresh / relaunch.`

issues.description: `restoreFromMnemonic()` still does `await agent.initialize({ recoveryPhrase: mnemonic })` and then immediately schedules `refreshIdentities()` (`src/lib/enbox/agent-store.ts:433-447`). The new retry code only retriggers once `hasAgentDid(agent)` becomes true (`src/lib/enbox/agent-store.ts:185-260,494-500`), but upstream does not assign `agentDid` during `initialize()` — it only does so in `EnboxUserAgent.start()` (`node_modules/@enbox/agent/dist/esm/enbox-user-agent.js:127-171`, also documented in `.factory/library/architecture.md:249-253`). Because the restore flow never calls `start()`, the poller simply hits its 2s cap and exits, leaving pre-existing identities stale/empty after RecoveryRestore. That means the prior scrutiny failure is still not fully addressed for one of the initialize-based call sites it originally flagged.`

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `mobile-feature-worker` honesty gate is still easy to violate silently: this session marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true` even though the transcript skeleton does not show the required `environment.md` read for test-running sessions and shows code edits before the new retry test file was created.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/mobile-feature-worker/SKILL.md:21,26-32` requires reading `.factory/library/environment.md` when running test flows and writing failing tests first. The transcript skeleton at `/home/liran/.factory/missions/08245e86-9e3a-49bb-ba11-c4ec0804b327/worker-transcripts.jsonl:97` shows reads of `mission.md`, `.factory/services.yaml`, `.factory/library/architecture.md`, and `AGENTS.md`, then an early `Execute` test run and `Edit` calls against `src/lib/enbox/agent-store.ts` before the later `Create` of `src/lib/enbox/__tests__/agent-store.refreshIdentities.retry.test.ts`; the handoff still records `"followedProcedure": true` at `/home/liran/.factory/missions/08245e86-9e3a-49bb-ba11-c4ec0804b327/handoffs/2026-04-24T06-56-41-189Z__fix-agent-did-race-retry-when-ready__76cc805c-f11d-4689-b424-49446f5d13d5.json:89-90`.

summary: Review failed. The new poller fixes the synthetic 'same agent later gets a DID' case, but `restoreFromMnemonic()` still never calls `start()`, so the retry has nothing to observe and the original initialize-path staleness remains for restored wallets. I also recorded that the worker again overstated `followedProcedure` relative to the skill's environment-read and test-first requirements.
